title: betula lyalliana
intro: Betula lyalliana, also known as Lyall's birch or mountain paper birch, is a species of tree in the family Betulaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is known for its papery white bark and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Betula lyalliana include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of Betula lyalliana include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions.
